body:
mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale
rationale: status: open
reason: retryable

Environment blocker: the Bash tool is completely unusable in this execution
worktree. Every invocation (including trivial commands like `true`, `echo`,
`ls`) fails immediately with:

  ENOSPC: no space left on device, mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/-Users-erik-Projects--ddx-exec-wt--execute-bead-wt-ddx-e07000c9-20260711T033713-29779ce8'

This happens before any user command runs -- it is the harness's own
sandbox/session scratch directory creation under /tmp/claude-501/ that is
failing, which means /tmp on this host is out of space or out of inodes.
This is the exact failure mode this bead (ddx-e07000c9) is filed to fix, so
it is plausible this worktree hit the live incident.

Non-Bash tools (Read, Write, Edit) work fine -- they don't need to create a
scratch dir under /tmp. I was able to read and analyze
cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go and confirm the root cause the bead
describes: defaultExecutionCleanupScratchPrefixes (execution_cleanup.go:26-44)
does not include "ddx-home-" or "ddx-fixture-bin-", so cleanupScratchRoots
(execution_cleanup.go:618) never matches those trees via hasAnyPrefix
(execution_cleanup.go:641) and they are never reclaimed.

I did not make the prefix-list edit because I have no way to run
`cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/...` or `lefthook run
pre-commit` to verify it, and no way to `git add`/`git commit` without Bash.
Committing unverified code would violate the "do not commit red code" rule
and the review gate.

Retry once /tmp on the host has free space/inodes again (or once this
worktree is rescheduled to a host where the Bash tool's scratch mkdir under
/tmp/claude-501/ succeeds). The fix itself is small and low-risk: add
"ddx-home-" and "ddx-fixture-bin-" to defaultExecutionCleanupScratchPrefixes
in cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go, then add the two tests named in
the bead's acceptance criteria (TestExecutionCleanupManager_ReclaimsStaleDDXHomeScratch
and TestExecutionCleanupSummary_ReportsScratchReclaimedInodes) alongside the
existing cleanup tests in cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_test.go,
following the existing scratch-dir test patterns in that file. The summary
already carries RemovedScratchDirs, ScratchBytesReclaimed, and
ScratchInodesReclaimed fields (execution_cleanup.go:154,164-165), so AC2
likely only needs a test asserting on those existing fields rather than new
plumbing -- but that needs confirming against the existing test file, which I
was not able to open in full without risking further Bash-dependent tooling.
</content>
